corrected_fields.summary: This Federal Reserve FAQ page explains how the FOMC collects and publishes economic projections (Summary of Economic Projections) from Board members and Reserve Bank presidents four times per year in March, June, September, and December. The projections include real GDP growth, unemployment rate, PCE inflation, and core PCE inflation forecasts, as well as participants' views on the appropriate target federal funds rate path.
corrected_fields.why_it_matters: This FAQ provides foundational context for understanding the Federal Reserve's Summary of Economic Projections (SEP), commonly known as the 'dot plot.' Market participants rely on SEP releases to gauge policymakers' views on appropriate monetary policy and economic trajectory. Knowing how to interpret ranges, central tendencies, and projection horizons is essential for following Fed communications.
corrected_fields.macro_context: This page explains the mechanics of how FOMC projections are structured and collected, which informs how markets interpret subsequent projection releases. The longer-run projections represent estimates of potential GDP growth, natural unemployment rate, and the inflation target consistent with the Fed's dual mandate.
